physics-double-slit: Create a Young double-slit diagram using only straight lines and nodes: source S at (-4,0), barrier vertical at x=-2 with two small slits S1 at y=0.5 and S2 at y=-0.5, screen vertical at x=4, point P at (4,1.2), central maximum O at (4,0). Draw rays S1-P and S2-P, slit separation d marker, path labels r1 and r2, and bright fringe ticks on screen. No decorations, no braces.
physics-electric-dipole: Create an electric dipole diagram using fixed coordinates: -q at (-1,0), +q at (1,0), dipole moment p arrow from -q to +q, field point P at (0,2.2), dashed distance line from O=(0,0) to P labeled r, angle theta between OP and vertical, and electric field arrow E at P. Use simple arcs only.
physics-rc-circuit: Create an RC circuit using plain TikZ only, no circuitikz and no to[...] syntax: rectangular loop, battery on left, switch on top, resistor rectangle R on top branch, capacitor two plates on right branch, current arrow I, capacitor labeled C, and voltage V label.
chem-titration-curve-weak-acid: Create a weak acid strong base titration curve with plain ASCII labels: axes Volume NaOH and pH, smooth S-shaped curve, buffer region label, half-equivalence point marker, equivalence point above pH 7, and endpoint region. Avoid special Unicode characters.
chem-le-chatelier-pressure: Create a Le Chatelier pressure diagram using plain text labels: container with piston pushing down, gas molecules N2, H2, NH3 inside, reaction label N2 + 3H2 <-> 2NH3, arrow labeled shift to NH3, and note fewer gas moles. Avoid Unicode arrows.
math-altitude-orthocenter: Create an acute triangle ABC with coordinates A=(0,0), B=(5,0), C=(2,3.5). Draw three dashed altitudes using simple explicit line segments, mark feet D,E,F, mark orthocenter H near (2,1.4), and label vertices. No coordinate calculations.
math-median-centroid: Create triangle ABC with A=(0,0), B=(5,0), C=(1.5,3.5). Mark midpoints D on BC, E on AC, F on AB. Draw medians AD, BE, CF as dashed lines meeting at centroid G. Label 2:1 on median AD. Use explicit coordinates only.
math-dilation-center: Create coordinate grid showing triangle A(1,1), B(2,1), C(1,2) dilated from origin O by scale factor 2 to A'(2,2), B'(4,2), C'(2,4). Draw rays from O through each original point to image point, label all points, and use straight segments only.
math-law-of-sines: Create triangle ABC with coordinates A=(0,0), B=(4,0), C=(1.4,2.8), draw circumcircle centered near (2,0.75) with radius 2.14, label sides a,b,c opposite A,B,C, draw a dashed diameter through C, and place formula a/sin A = b/sin B = c/sin C below.
